orgDesign.companyProfile: Scaled software organization with many stream-aligned product teams plus specialist data, ML, reliability, and platform groups. Platform engineering is modeled as an internal product organization serving product and technology teams.
orgDesign.operatingModel: Platform teams own reusable internal products and golden paths, while stream-aligned teams own customer-facing outcomes. Enablement work is time-bound and exists to move teams back onto self-service paths rather than become a permanent consulting layer.
designPrinciples: Treat internal product and technology teams as customers, not as passive consumers of central standards.
Keep the platform thin enough to reduce cognitive load and avoid becoming an opaque mandatory layer.
Use enablement and exception handling to improve the paved road, not to replace it.
orgDesign.staffingPrinciple: Prefer teams of roughly 7-10 FTE with a product manager or platform product owner where the customer surface is meaningful and recurring.
orgDesign.groupingPrinciple: Group teams by platform product area so ownership, service levels, and lifecycle decisions stay explicit.
orgDesign.groupLeadershipPrinciple: Use lightweight group-level leadership with strong staff-plus engineering presence rather than oversizing central architecture functions.

groups.name: Developer Experience Platform
groups.mission: Own the front door to the platform and the paved-road experience that makes adoption discoverable, usable, and measurable.

teams.name: Platform Portal and Catalog
teams.mission: Own the developer portal, service catalog, and workflow entry points that connect internal teams to the platform.

relatedKPIs: Time from repo creation to first production deployment

staffing.staffingGuidance: Needs strong product thinking and enough backend and UX capacity to keep workflows coherent and supportable.

roles.responsibility: Own portal and catalog priorities, adoption goals, and customer research.
Lead delivery quality, staffing, and platform reliability for the team.
Set technical direction for portal architecture and platform integrations.
Build metadata services, workflow adapters, and APIs.
Build the portal experience and user-facing workflows.
Own usability, information architecture, and workflow clarity.

teams.name: Platform Golden Paths
teams.mission: Own templates, supported service patterns, and migration guidance for mainstream engineering paths.

relatedKPIs: Time from repo creation to first production deployment
Time from repo creation to first production deployment

supportingProductBricks.brickName: Artifact Supply Chain and Base Images
Policy as Code and Compliance Controls

staffing.staffingGuidance: Needs strong software engineering and platform migration capability because the quality of templates determines platform adoption.

roles.responsibility: Own template portfolio, migration priorities, and product-team feedback loops.
Lead delivery and team health.
Guard long-term template, automation, and migration architecture.
Build and maintain templates, scaffolding, and validation harnesses.
Keep supported paths and migration guidance understandable.
Own template regression depth and reference-service validation.

groups.name: Runtime and Delivery Foundation
groups.mission: Own environment, deployment, and connectivity foundations that most product teams should use by default.

teams.name: Platform Environment Provisioning
teams.mission: Own environment creation, ephemeral environment lifecycle, and runtime baseline provisioning.

relatedKPIs: Time from repo creation to first production deployment
Shared-foundation reuse across specialist platform teams

staffing.staffingGuidance: Needs a mix of cloud infrastructure, platform API, and runtime operations expertise.

roles.responsibility: Own provisioning product scope, usage tradeoffs, and adoption.
Lead delivery and operational health.
Set technical direction for environment control-plane design.
Build provisioning workflows, APIs, and infrastructure modules.
Guard reliability and rollout safety of provisioning workflows.
Track environment economics and right-size usage policies.

teams.name: Platform Delivery Automation
teams.mission: Own the shared delivery path, including build, test, deploy, promotion, rollback, and release evidence workflows.

relatedKPIs: Time from repo creation to first production deployment
Time from repo creation to first production deployment

supportingProductBricks.brickName: Artifact Supply Chain and Base Images
Policy as Code and Compliance Controls

staffing.staffingGuidance: Needs strong automation depth and enough customer contact to keep pipeline standards usable.

roles.responsibility: Own the common delivery path and release workflow priorities.
Lead delivery execution and team health.
Set technical direction for CI/CD architecture and controls.
Build and maintain delivery automation, shared runners, and release tooling.
Validate the delivery path through reference-service testing and rollout checks.

teams.name: Platform Runtime Connectivity
teams.mission: Own approved connectivity, dependency, and runtime access patterns across the shared platform foundation.

relatedKPIs: Time from repo creation to first production deployment
Shared-foundation reuse across specialist platform teams

staffing.staffingGuidance: Needs strong networking, runtime, and integration-pattern expertise to keep the platform comprehensible and supportable.

roles.responsibility: Own connectivity standards, reference patterns, and customer tradeoffs.
Lead delivery and operations.
Set technical direction for runtime connectivity and contracts.
Implement connectivity controls, runtime integrations, and reference patterns.
Own runtime diagnostics and connectivity incident patterns.

groups.name: Platform Security and Reliability
groups.mission: Embed controls, telemetry, and recovery mechanisms into the normal platform path so teams inherit strong defaults.

teams.name: Platform Identity and Guardrails
teams.mission: Own workload identity, secrets, policy automation, trusted artifacts, and exception workflows.

ownedProductBricks.brickName: Policy as Code and Compliance Controls
Artifact Supply Chain and Base Images

staffing.staffingGuidance: Needs a balanced mix of platform engineering, security, and product skills because the goal is safe adoption, not only control enforcement.

roles.responsibility: Own control-baseline priorities and exception-product design.
Lead delivery quality and operational health.
Set technical direction for identity, policy, and artifact trust.
Build identity, secret, policy, and artifact workflows.
Translate control evidence and governance needs into automated workflows.
Integrate guardrails into templates, pipelines, and runtime automation.

teams.name: Platform Observability and Reliability
teams.mission: Own platform telemetry, service-health models, incident automation, and recovery workflows across the shared estate.

relatedKPIs: Incident MTTR for common platform failure classes
Incident MTTR for common platform failure classes

staffing.staffingGuidance: Needs enough operational depth to keep telemetry and response systems trustworthy during incidents.

roles.responsibility: Own telemetry and incident product priorities with strong customer focus.
Lead delivery and operational health.
Set technical direction for telemetry, alerting, and automation architecture.
Build and operate observability, alerting, and incident systems.
Integrate telemetry and incident workflows across platform products.
Model incident, alert-quality, and reliability outcome metrics.

groups.name: Platform Insights and Enablement
groups.mission: Measure platform value, guide migrations, and keep internal product decisions grounded in evidence and customer contact.

teams.name: Platform Insights and FinOps
teams.mission: Own platform usage analytics, DevEx metrics, cost attribution, and value reporting.

relatedKPIs: Incident MTTR for common platform failure classes

staffing.staffingGuidance: Keep this team analytically strong and tightly connected to platform product decisions rather than treating reporting as an afterthought.

roles.responsibility: Own insights priorities, metric definitions, and leadership reporting.
Lead data modeling and metrics architecture.
Build DevEx, platform-value, and FinOps data products.
Translate usage and infrastructure cost into team-level economic signals.
Interpret platform outcomes and support discovery and prioritization.

teams.name: Platform Enablement and Field Engineering
teams.mission: Help teams adopt the platform, close high-value gaps, and feed real customer evidence back into platform product decisions.

relatedKPIs: Shared-foundation reuse across specialist platform teams

staffing.staffingGuidance: Keep this team explicitly time-bound and migration-oriented so it strengthens the platform instead of becoming permanent custom delivery labor.

roles.responsibility: Own adoption strategy, migration support, and customer feedback loops.
Pair with teams on onboarding, migration, and platform issue diagnosis.
Coordinate high-value migrations and cross-team dependencies.
Capture customer evidence and translate it into platform opportunities.
